author: m.kalinin
content: I might not have enough context, but i would not overcomplicate. IMHO, we could use `0xB0` pre-fork and post-fork and at the same time make this prefix the start of the builder prefixes range to be used in the future

author: jtraglia
content: That’s not what I’m asking about there. Payload builders are v0. Should we allow v1 builders (unknown right now) to submit execution payload bids before we know what they are? I have a feeling we’re going to want to use the builder registry when we introduce mandatory proofs. If that means a new v1 builder for proofs, we’re going to have to revert this change to disallow them from submitting execution payload bids. I believe potuz wants to do this on the off-chance a new builder version needs to submit a bid while still in Gloas, before the fork in which the new builder version is defined.

author: tersec
content: Deeply skeptical of this, just allowing unknown semantics into a protocol defined by consensus

author: tersec
content: Maximal flexibility now directly means minimal flexibility in the future

author: tersec
content: Because it closes off design space

author: jtraglia
content: Could you clarify what “this” is? Do you think we should or should not allow v1 builders to submit payload bids?

author: potuz
content: <@586161934425128960> I opened this which goes in the opposite direciton of yours https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5429, explanation in the PR. cc <@592004585506340885> cause you requested Nico's PR.

author: nflaig
content:
```
- _[IGNORE]_ `bid.parent_block_hash` is the block hash of a known execution
  payload in fork choice and
  `is_gas_limit_target_compatible(parent_gas_limit, bid.gas_limit, proposer_preferences.target_gas_limit)`
  is `True` where `parent_gas_limit` is the `gas_limit` of that execution
  payload.
```
having this as 2 separate conditions would still be good imo

author: potuz
content: yeah I assume with Justin's work on executable p2p stack also it may help to have them separated
